SystemAndRoleList-Documentation.FullQualifiedDomainName: REQUIRED: FQDN of system. Example: cm01.contoso.local
SystemAndRoleList-Documentation.IPAddress:: REQUIRED: One or more IP addresses of the FullQualifiedDomainName separated via comma. The script will run a DNS lookup in case this field was left blank
SystemAndRoleList-Documentation.Description: OPTIONAL: A description for the server to make it easier to identify any system in this config file
SystemAndRoleList-Documentation.SiteCode: SiteCode of the system. Used to match roles to the correct site. To prevent a DP of Site A to connect to Site B for example
SystemAndRoleList-Documentation.ParentSiteCode: SiteCode of the parent site system. Could be a CAS in case of a Primary Site or a Primary Site on case of a Secondary Site
RoleList: REQUIRED: List of roles. A role defines what firewallrules will be applied to a system.
A role could be 'Webserver' for example to be able to define port 80 or 443 in a firewall rule
Define your own rules if necessary and use them in a 'RuleDefinition' (Explained further down)
ConfigMgrConsole = Systems running the ConfigMgr console
SiteServer = Site Server wether CAS, PRI or SEC site
SMSProvider = System with SMS Provider role installed
ManagementPoint = System with Management Point role installed
DistributionPoint = System with Distribution Point role installed
EndpointProtectionPoint = System with Endpoint Protection Point role installed
PrimarySoftwareUpdatePoint = The first Software Update Point all other SUPs use
SoftwareUpdatePoint = System with Software Update Point role installed
ReportingServicePoint = System with Reporting Service Point role installed
PRISQLServerRole = SQL Server of Primary Site
SUPSQLServerRole = SQL Server of Software Update Point
CMGConnectionPoint = System with CMG Connection Point role installed
ServiceConnectionPoint = System with Service Connection Point role installed
FallbackStatusPoint = System with Fallback Status Point role installed
StateMigrationPoint = System with State Migration Point role installed
DataWarehouseServicePoint = System with Data Warehouse Service Point role installed
CertificateRegistrationPoint = System with Certificate Registration Point role installed
ContentLibraryServer = System where a remote Content Library is stored
ContentSourceServer = System used as the content source server
ExternalSQLConnection = External system to ConfigMgr with the need to connect to SQL server
RDPSystem = System with the need to connect via RDP to ConfigMgr systems
SMBSystem = System with the need to connect via SMB to ConfigMgr systems

RuleDefinition-Documentation.Source: Source role name from the RoleList of systems. Could be ConfigMgrConsole or PrimarySite for example
RuleDefinition-Documentation.Destination: Destination role name from the RoleList of systems. Could be ConfigMgrConsole or PrimarySite for example
RuleDefinition-Documentation.Direction: Rule direction. Either Inbound or Outbound
RuleDefinition-Documentation.Profile: Either Domain, Private, Public or Any
RuleDefinition-Documentation.Group: Name if the firewall rule group to be able to group rules together
RuleDefinition-Documentation.IgnoreSiteCode: If set to True the rule will not stick to the sitecode. Can be used to allow a rule for all Distribution Points instead of just the ones of a specific site for example
Services: List fo services from the ServiceDefinition of this file. Example:

ServiceDefinition-Documentation.Name: Name of a service. Used in the services list of a firewall rule.
ServiceDefinition-Documentation.Port: Port or system defined name like RPC, RPCUDP or RPCDynamic
ServiceDefinition-Documentation.Program: Path to a program the rule applies to. Double backslash in path required. Example: %systemroot%\system32\svchost.exe
ServiceDefinition-Documentation.Description: Description of the service. Not used in the rule, for for the config file

SystemAndRoleList.Description: Main Primary Site without MP and DP

RuleDefinition.Description: Required for site server high availability (active/passive site server). Both nodes share the same site code.

RuleDefinition.RuleName: ConfigMgr Asset Intelligence Synchronization Point to Internet
RuleDefinition.Description: Asset Intelligence sync point downloads catalog data from Microsoft. NOTE: Destination 'Internet' is not a real system. Rule listed for completeness. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/configmgr/core/plan-design/hierarchy/ports#asset-intelligence-synchronization-point--microsoft

RuleDefinition.RuleName: ConfigMgr Endpoint Protection Point to Internet
RuleDefinition.Description: Endpoint Protection point downloads definition updates from the internet. NOTE: Destination 'Internet' is not a real system. Rule listed for completeness. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/configmgr/core/plan-design/hierarchy/ports#endpoint-protection-point--internet

RuleDefinition.RuleName: ConfigMgr Console to Internet
RuleDefinition.Description: Console accesses internet for software update downloads, feedback, documentation links and Community hub. NOTE: Destination 'Internet' is not a real system. Rule listed for completeness. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/configmgr/core/plan-design/hierarchy/ports#configuration-manager-console--internet

RuleDefinition.RuleName: ConfigMgr Software Update Point to Internet
RuleDefinition.Description: Software update point synchronizes update metadata and content from Microsoft Update. Can be routed through a configured proxy server. NOTE: Destination 'Internet' is not a real system. Rule listed for completeness. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/configmgr/core/plan-design/hierarchy/ports#software-update-point--internet

RuleDefinition.RuleName: ConfigMgr Service Connection Point to Azure (Microsoft cloud)
RuleDefinition.Description: Service connection point connects to Azure/Microsoft cloud services for CMG deployment, Azure Logic App external notifications, telemetry, hotfix download, etc. NOTE: Destination 'Internet' is not a real system. Rule listed for completeness. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/configmgr/core/plan-design/hierarchy/ports#service-connection-point--azure-cmg

RuleDefinition.RuleName: ConfigMgr Site Server to Internet
RuleDefinition.Description: Site server connects to internet for product/component updates and Microsoft cloud services. Can be routed through a configured proxy server. NOTE: Destination 'Internet' is not a real system. Rule listed for completeness. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/configmgr/core/plan-design/hierarchy/ports#site-server--internet

RuleDefinition.RuleName: ConfigMgr Site Server to Cloud Distribution Point
RuleDefinition.Description: Site server uploads content to Cloud Distribution Point in Azure. NOTE: Destination 'Internet' is not a real system. Rule listed for completeness. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/configmgr/core/plan-design/hierarchy/ports#site-server--cloud-distribution-point

RuleDefinition.RuleName: ConfigMgr CMG Connection Point to CMG cloud service
RuleDefinition.Description: CMG connection point builds the CMG channel to the CMG VM scale set / classic cloud service in Azure. HTTPS 443 plus 10124-10139 (VMSS / HTTPS fallback) and 10140-10155 (TCP-TLS preferred for classic). NOTE: Destination 'Internet' is not a real system. Rule listed for completeness. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/configmgr/core/plan-design/hierarchy/ports#cmg-connection-point--cmg-virtual-machine-scale-set

RuleDefinition.RuleName: ConfigMgr Client to Cloud Distribution Point
RuleDefinition.Description: Clients download content from Cloud Distribution Point in Azure. NOTE: Destination 'Internet' is not a real system. Rule listed for completeness. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/configmgr/core/plan-design/hierarchy/ports#client--cloud-distribution-point

RuleDefinition.RuleName: ConfigMgr Client to Cloud Management Gateway (CMG)
RuleDefinition.Description: Internet-based clients communicate with the Cloud Management Gateway in Azure. NOTE: Destination 'Internet' is not a real system. Rule listed for completeness. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/configmgr/core/plan-design/hierarchy/ports#client--cloud-management-gateway-cmg

ServiceDefinition.Name: CMGVMSS
ServiceDefinition.Description: CMG channel to VM scale set with two or more VMs (also HTTPS fallback for classic CMG)

ServiceDefinition.Name: CMGClassicTCPTLS
ServiceDefinition.Description: CMG channel to classic cloud service via TCP-TLS (preferred)
